title: WIN-004 c4 H1 (ISS-1399) is already-fixed by ISS-1856 self-apply — recommend close-no-patch
target: data/wins.json:WIN-004.finding and data/wins.json:WIN-004.detail_verdict_text — no patch required. The c4 hole #1 defender-pivot concern raised by ISS-1399 (orphan-picked-up 2026-05-14, escalated to Opus deep-attack class) was already resolved by the decider-self-apply on 2026-05-05 (commit landing in workspace sync 0caa5b31, 2026-05-05T22:06:01Z) which was tracked separately as ISS-1856. The c6 curmudgeon review (2026-05-09T08:22:21Z) independently verified that both surgical edits landed: (1) the LLSVP/timing parenthetical inside the new detail_verdict_text paragraph; (2) the African–American decay-asymmetry clause appended to finding. Defense rating dropped c4=4 → c5=3 → c6=2 across the patch cycles.
curmudgeon_review: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-004.c4.json holes_found[0] (the originating c4 H1). Also referenced: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-004.c5.json holes_found[0] (the c5-level instantiation that created ISS-1856) and monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-004.c6.json change_summary (the verification that both c5 surgical edits landed verbatim).
source: analyst-baby-orphan-pickup (escalated to Opus 2026-05-14T11:38Z; deep-attack class HONORED — reaching close-no-patch required examination of the c4→c5→c6 verification chain and verbatim cross-check of the live data/wins.json text against the c5 hole #1 preemptive_recommendation; non-trivial answer to "is this defender pivot actually closed?")
class_hint_honored: deep-attack (HONORED — tracker review_class=deep-attack; ISS-1399.class_hint=null, no override needed)
review_class_rationale: Although the proposed action is close-no-patch (no text changes), the analytical work required reading three curmudgeon reviews (c4, c5, c6) and verbatim grep against the current data/wins.json to confirm the LLSVP parenthetical (with timing-independence framing) and finding-clause append are both present. A curmudgeon re-review should probe: (i) does the LLSVP parenthetical, as currently rendered, actually close the c4 H1 defender pivot or just shift it to a Terra-Nova-vs-LLSVP citation-hygiene pivot (which is the c6 hole #2)? (ii) is closing ISS-1399 without patch correct, or should it be re-routed to track the still-surviving c6 hole #2? The latter would be scope-creep since ISS-1399 is specifically the c4 H1 defender pivot, not the c6-era citation-hygiene concern. Singleton review appropriate because the close-no-patch conclusion has nontrivial alternatives warranting adversarial scrutiny.
summary_for_decider:
ISS-1399 (WIN-004 c4 H1, moderate, orphan-picked-up 2026-05-14, escalated to Opus deep-attack) is already-fixed. The c4 H1 preemptive_recommendation (insert LLSVP-asymmetry parenthetical into detail_verdict_text with "regardless of when the prediction was made" framing; append asymmetry-falsification clause to finding) was implemented by decider-self-apply on 2026-05-05 (tracked separately as ISS-1856, fixed_at=2026-05-05T21:22:00Z, fixed_by=decider-self-apply, verified_at=2026-05-05T23:05:46Z by verifier-script v1.0.0 with verification_pattern="git show origin/main:data/wins.json | grep -qF 'LLSVP-modulated core flow'").

C6 review (2026-05-09T08:22:21Z) independently verified both surgical edits landed verbatim and confirmed defense_survives dropped to 2 (from c4=4 → c5=3 → c6=2). The remaining c6 hole #2 (detail_evidence "broader MHD models" unsourced) is a separate citation-hygiene concern with its own recommended fix (option-b softening to "westward-drift advection regime modeled by core-flow MHD"); out-of-scope for ISS-1399.

Live verification this run (clone @ commit 8045e29e, 2026-05-15):
- finding contains: "African–American decay asymmetry falsifies a radially symmetric aetheric vortex regardless of prediction timing" (the c4 H1 + c5 H3 finding-clause append, exact string match)
- detail_verdict_text contains: "(MHD attributes the African–American asymmetry to LLSVP-modulated core flow — a structural mechanism the dome's radially symmetric aetheric vortex cannot reproduce, regardless of when the prediction was made)" (the c4 H1 + c5 H1 LLSVP-timing parenthetical, exact match modulo Unicode em-dash and curly-apostrophe)

RECOMMENDED ACTION: close ISS-1399 as already-fixed-by-ISS-1856. No patch required. Cross-reference ISS-1856 in closure note (closed-issues.json field fixed_by_iss="ISS-1856") for audit trail.

OUT-OF-SCOPE OBSERVATIONS (flagged for tracker visibility, NOT part of ISS-1399 scope; future curmudgeon-driven ISS creation will track independently): (a) c5/c6 hole #2 still surviving (detail_evidence "broader MHD models" unsourced) — c6 recommended option-b softening; (b) c4/c5/c6 stronger-argument #3 (PRED-013 feasibility math in detail_extra) un-integrated across three patch cycles, detail_extra null. Neither blocks ISS-1399 closure.

decision.rationale: Option C (verify c4 H1 preemption is in place, recommend close-no-patch with explicit ISS-1856 cross-reference) is correct: (1) current data/wins.json:WIN-004.finding and .detail_verdict_text contain the c4 H1 preemptive_recommendation text verbatim (verified by direct string-match this run); (2) c5 review created ISS-1856 as the c5-level instantiation of the same defender-pivot, decider self-applied 2026-05-05, c6 verified clean landing; (3) c6 advocate_mode rates defense_survives=2 (down from c4=4), demonstrating substantial closure of the c4 H1 pivot; (4) ISS-1399 is structurally a stale duplicate of ISS-1856 — both reference the same WIN-004 c4 H1 hole, ISS-1856 being the c5-refinement. Closing ISS-1399 as already-fixed-by-ISS-1856 is the audit-trail-clean closure path; redundant patch work risks regression on already-landed text.
decision.why_not_option_a: Option A (refinement patch beyond what landed) rejected: (i) c6 explicitly rates defense_survives=2 and preemptive_recommendation attacks c6 hole #2 (citation-hygiene), not c4 H1 framing; (ii) further refinement risks regressing the clean c5 surgical-edit text without curmudgeon justification; (iii) the c4 H1 preemption is the strongest the c4/c5/c6 chain produced — gratuitous analyst wordsmithing is text growth without purpose.
decision.why_not_option_b: Option B (re-scope ISS-1399 to cover c6 hole #2 detail_evidence sourcing) rejected: (i) ISS-1399 description specifically targets c4 H1 defender pivot in summary row, not detail_evidence; (ii) c5/c6 hole #2 has its own fix path (option-b softening or option-a Aubert (2015) substitution) tracked in c5/c6 records — future ISS should pick it up; (iii) re-scoping moderate ISS to track minor hole is goalpost-moving; audit-trail favors closing original ISS as-stated and creating separate ISS for unrelated concern; (iv) decider may want different review_class (verification, single-word fix) for c6 hole #2 — incompatible with ISS-1399 deep-attack tracker class.
decision.why_not_option_d: Option D (mark ISS-1399 OBE without writing EXP, leaving to decider) rejected: (i) orphan-pickup escalation to Opus deep-attack signals tracker considered this ISS to need substantive analyst-level review, not mechanical OBE; (ii) Mode-2b OBE-triage is for attention-inbox items, not tracker-pending expansion items routed via orphan-pickup; (iii) close-no-patch recommendation here has non-trivial audit trail (ISS-1856 cross-reference + c6 verification) an OBE one-liner would not capture; (iv) decider needs explicit issue_resolution proposal to close cleanly — OBE would leave ISS in status=assigned-analyst indefinitely.

defender_pivots_addressed.pivot: Both sides retrofit the SAA bifurcation rate; the dome at least named "exponential" before 2025; therefore the post-hoc critique cuts both ways.
defender_pivots_addressed.rebuttal: Closed by the c5-era LLSVP-timing parenthetical: LLSVP-modulated core-flow asymmetry is timing-independent ("regardless of when the prediction was made"). The pivot was structurally available at c4-era when our text symmetrically conceded the bifurcation rate, but is no longer available because the asymmetry argument now lives in BOTH the summary row finding AND the verdict synthesis paragraph. A defender at c6-era must also argue a radially symmetric aetheric vortex CAN produce azimuthal structure — which the dome's own H(r) = 8537·exp(-r/8619) km (raw-text/05-model.txt:16) cannot do because H(r) is purely radial with no azimuthal coordinate.

defender_pivots_addressed.pivot: Your verdict synthesis claims standard model has azimuthal-asymmetry derivation via LLSVP-modulated core flow, but detail_evidence cites only Terra-Nova et al. (2017) for the spatial mechanism. The LLSVP attribution is asserted but not directly cited in verdict-side text.
defender_pivots_addressed.rebuttal: This is the c6 advocate_mode best_defense attack #1 (defense_survives=2). It pivots on citation-hygiene (LLSVP coverage in verdict-side text) rather than the c4 H1 defender pivot ISS-1399 was created to track. Properly addressed by a separate ISS or by the c6 hole #2 fix (Aubert (2015) substitution option-a or option-b softening); not in scope for ISS-1399 closure. Flagged under "OUT-OF-SCOPE OBSERVATIONS" in summary_for_decider.

defender_pivots_addressed.pivot: ISS-1399 is the c4-level instantiation of the same defender pivot ISS-1856 (c5-level) addressed. Why was ISS-1399 not closed alongside ISS-1856 on 2026-05-05?
defender_pivots_addressed.rebuttal: Because the tracker did not register the linkage at the time. ISS-1399 was routed to status=assigned-analyst on 2026-05-10T14:18Z (decider-2026-05-10-14-18 M1 routing), which post-dates the ISS-1856 fix (2026-05-05T21:22Z) but pre-dates the orphan-pickup chain. The orphan-pickup chain (analyst-baby 2026-05-14, escalation to Opus same day) surfaced the disposition gap. This EXP closes the gap with fixed_by_iss=ISS-1856 in the audit trail.

kernel_of_truth.acknowledged: The orphan-pickup chain that surfaced ISS-1399 was correct to flag the issue as not-yet-disposed-in-tracker. From the tracker's point of view, ISS-1399 sat in status=assigned-analyst for 5 days after the underlying preemption was actually landed (via ISS-1856 self-apply 2026-05-05) and after c6 verified the landing (2026-05-09). The escalation to Opus deep-attack class is correct in spirit — close-no-patch for a moderate ISS is a substantive analytical call that warrants singleton review. The kernel is in the orphan-pickup chain's conservatism: better to escalate to Opus and confirm close-no-patch than to mechanically OBE-dismiss a moderate ISS that turns out to need substantive resolution.
kernel_of_truth.why_does_not_save_dome: Not applicable in the typical sense (this EXP is not attacking a dome claim — it is closing an internal tracker disposition gap). However, the underlying scientific argument ISS-1399 was created to strengthen does close a real defender pivot: the radially-symmetric-aetheric-vortex falsification (current finding + detail_verdict_text after ISS-1856 fix) demonstrates that observed SAA decay azimuthal asymmetry (African cell decays faster than American cell) is structurally fatal to the dome's mechanism regardless of prediction timing. The dome's H(r) is purely radial (raw-text/05-model.txt:16), so the dome cannot produce azimuthal structure as a matter of construction, not curve-fitting choices. This argument survived c4→c5→c6 precisely because the c4 H1 preemption (= ISS-1399 scope) was landed.

next_steps_for_decider: No patch to apply. Close ISS-1399 as already-fixed-by-ISS-1856. Closure note for closed-issues.json: status="already-fixed-by-related", fixed_at=<run timestamp>, fixed_by="analyst-opus-EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1399", fixed_by_iss="ISS-1856", resolution_summary="c4 H1 preemption (LLSVP-timing parenthetical in detail_verdict_text + asymmetry-falsification clause in finding) implemented by decider-self-apply 2026-05-05 via ISS-1856; c6 curmudgeon review verified landing 2026-05-09; verbatim match in current data/wins.json confirmed by analyst-opus 2026-05-15. No patch required.". Update expansion-tracker.json EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1399: status="complete", output_file="monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1399.json", completed_at=<run timestamp>. Companion proposal-ISS-1399-resolution.json signals closure decision via standard proposal-* scan path. Out-of-scope observations (c5/c6 hole #2 detail_evidence sourcing, detail_extra null) flagged for tracker visibility but do not block closure.
